stemHtml: An aircraft with a conventional, cambered aerofoil accelerates through the transonic speed range. Shock waves form on the upper and lower surfaces of the wing. While the aircraft continues to accelerate, the shock wave on the lower surface initially moves aft…

options.a: more quickly than the upper shock wave.
options.b: but remains ahead of the upper shock wave.
options.c: directly beneath the upper shock wave and at the same rate.
options.d: to the location of the upper shock wave, then remains static.
